violations.text: Open employees' beverage containers were observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Containers were removed during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

A sponge was observed in the kitchen hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Sponge was removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of pink biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Machine taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized; ice disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026
An employee's cell phone was observed on a cutting board. Phone was removed, cutting board was taken to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

Beef was observed thawing on top of the chest freezer. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. PIC placed beef in walk-in. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

Working containers of water and oil are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026
A deceased rodent was observed behind water heaters. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026
Cut strawberries were observed in a re-used single service container. PIC placed strawberries in a hotel pan, container disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026
The dumpster was observed filled with excess garbage. Per PIC, dumpster is scheduled to be emptied today. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a mop and bucket, bus pan, and dish cloth. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. All items were moved during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Strawberries were observed with mold-like growth. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Raw animal foods (eggs, sausage) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (produce) in the walk-in refrigerator and prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC moved items during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Potatoes, which were prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Potatoes and sausage were observed held at 128- and 98-degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
The prep unit was observed holding an ambient temperature of 45 degrees. PIC adjusted temperature settings; observed ambient at 40 degrees. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2025

A can of sauce and container of fryer oil were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Items were moved during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
A case of single service items were observed stored on the floor in the basement. PIC placed on shelf. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Non-food grade containers were observed being used to store prepared foods. PIC informed to cease use. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2025
A leak was observed at the flush handle of the urinal. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2025

The dumpster outside is located on dirt and gravel. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2025
A hole was observed in the tile in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2025
